summary: tests fail if retry_writes is true

summary: Improve BSON error messages
description:
See DRIVERS-281 but necessary changes for Perl driver:

* Report bad field types in hex notation.
* Add a note to the error that users should check for a newer driver.
* If possible ("SHOULD") report the field name in the error message. 
* -Backport this change to the 0.x driver.-

Reporting the field name will be tricky. This probably means throwing the exception higher up the stack where the field name is available.  This is tricky for arrays as knowing that element 3 of some unknown array had a bad type isn't very useful.  Possibly we could cascade an error string upwards with field names being prepended, but that seems fragile.

summary: Improve BSON::XS error messages
description:
See DRIVERS-281 but necessary changes for Perl driver:

* Report bad field types in hex notation.
* Add a note to the error that users should check for a newer driver.

Reporting the field name will be tricky. This probably means throwing the exception higher up the stack where the field name is available.  This is tricky for arrays as knowing that element 3 of some unknown array had a bad type isn't very useful.  Possibly we could cascade an error string upwards with field names being prepended, but that seems fragile.

summary: Error at connecting when using mongodb+srv style URI
description:
When using something like this.

$mongodb = MongoDB::MongoClient->new( 'host' => mongodb+srv://....../

                                                                           .... )

You will receive the following or similar error:

Value "1567804623.2687" did not pass type constraint "Int" (in $self->\{"expires"}) at /usr/local/share/perl5/MongoDB/_URI.pm line 599

The reason is obvious:

In the begining of _URI.pm file built in time funtion is over defined by Time::Hires time function.

Perl built in time function returns integers but Time:HiRes module time function return fractions as seconds.

Possible FIX: 

remove this line from _URI.pm

use Time::HiRes qw(time);

summary: Error at connecting when using mongodb+srv style URI and boolean parameters
description:
$mongodb = MongoDB::MongoClient->new( 'host' => mongodb+srv://....../?ssl=true

                                                                           .... )

You will receive the following or similar error:

expected boolean string 'true' or 'false' for key 'ssl' but instead received '0'. Ignoring 'ssl'.

Instead of ssl parameter, the error will occur with any boolean parameter.

The reason is that in _URI.pm when connection URI contains mongodb+srv style protocol, sub _parse_srv_uri  pre-parses the URI and call __str_to_bool for each boolean parameters, It transforms each true/false value strings to 1/0 values correspondingly. So when after this pre-parsing phase, the normal  parsing phase occurs in sub _initialize_from_uri and __str_to_bool is called again for each boolean parameter if fails because all boolean parameters are already trasformed to 0/1 but __str_to_bool only accepts true/false as boolean value.

Possible quick FIX: 

alter sub __str_to_bool to accept values true/false and 1/0 for boolean parameters:

or alter sub _parse_srv_uri not to transform boolean parameter strings but leave them as-is.

summary: MongoDB::BulkWriteResult has incorrect elements in inserted[]
description:
Hello,

If `insert_many()` gets exception `MongoDB::DuplicateKeyError` with `write_errors` for some documents and `inserted` for another documents in the same bulk, then `inserted` array contains hashrefs with incorrect `_id`'s of successfully inserted documents.

Sample code to run is attached. Look for lines _BUGBUG_.

summary: BSON::XS does not compile anymore on newer fedora systems
description:
It seems that fedora has now its own gettid() function breaking the one provided by BSON::XS (or so). While building the distribution:
{noformat}cc -c -I. -Ibson -fwrapv -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe -fstack-protector-strong -I/usr/local/include -D_LARGEFILE_SOURCE -D_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64 -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 -D_GNU_SOURCE -DMONGO_USE_64_BIT_INT -DBSON_COMPILATION -O2 -DVERSION=\"v0.8.1\" -DXS_VERSION=\"v0.8.1\" -o bson/bson-context.o -fPIC "-I/opt/perl-5.31.6/lib/5.31.6/x86_64-linux/CORE" bson/bson-context.c
bson/bson-context.c:51:1: error: conflicting types for ‘gettid’
51 | gettid (void)
| ^~~~~~
In file included from /usr/include/unistd.h:1170,
from bson/bson-compat.h:65,
from bson/bson-context.c:17:
/usr/include/bits/unistd_ext.h:34:16: note: previous declaration of ‘gettid’ was here
34 | extern __pid_t gettid (void) __THROW;
| ^~~~~~
make: *** [Makefile:348: bson/bson-context.o] Error 1
{noformat}

Full sample report on cpantesters: http://www.cpantesters.org/cpan/report/b9c7a518-1353-11ea-be78-471e1f24ea8f
Summary of reports --- currently all UNKNOWN reports come from my fedora31 smoker: http://matrix.cpantesters.org/?dist=BSON-XS%20v0.8.1;os=linux;reports=1#sl=0,1
(Note that the kernel here still says ".fc28.", but lsb_release says it is a fedora31)

summary: URI sanitization could be improved
TLS URI options have no effect
description: "tls*" options were added to URI parsing, but not wired up to MongoClient.

summary: BulkWriteView doesn't support update pipelines
Memory leak in BSON::XS in the C code
Do not enforce batch size for collection and index enumeration commands
description:
1.6.0-RC3 currently enforces a batch size of zero, which resulted in the first batch always being empty (highlighting the bug in PHP-1355).

For server 2.8.0-RC3, where cursor responses are optional (based on a {{cursor}} option being present), [this commit|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/35f5da4152179c0b8c1b091633499eca0def4c7a] included in PHP-1321 ensures that we handle both inline command and cursor responses. 2.8.0-RC4+ will always use cursor responses, regardless of whether the command document contains a {{cursor}} option (or a requested batch size). If we remove batch size enforcement, we can instead rely on the server to choose an optimal batch size.

Note: although we do have a {{MONGO_DEFAULT_COMMAND_BATCH_SIZE}} constant (defined as 101), that was made with aggregation in mind and may not necessarily be the same batch size used for collection and index enumeration cursors. The command spec is silent on this.

summary: Do not compare php_mongocursor_is_valid() result to FAILURE constant
Collection and index enumeration fails if cursor's first batch is empty
description: Discovered while testing 2.8.0-RC4. The existing code enforced a batch size of zero, which caused the first batch to always be empty.

summary: Command cursor may cause log_response_header callback to segfault
description: If the command cursor was constructed from a raw command result (e.g. {{MongoCommandCursor::createFromDocument()}}, it will not have a query. This will cause the {{log_response_header}} callback invocation to segfault.

summary: Allow command cursor option to be array or object
description: When invoking a raw command (using {{MongoDB::command()}}), the {{cursor}} option must be a BSON object if it is present; however, {{php_mongo_enforce_batch_size_on_command()}} requires it to be an array. This is problematic when the user wants to specify an empty BSON object as the cursor option (i.e. to allow the server's default batch size to be used), as an empty PHP array would not be serialized as an empty BSON object.

summary: php_mongo_runcommand() should load current element before handling errors
aggregateCursor() with $out does not force primary selection
description: The purpose of this issue is to make {{aggregateCursor()}} consistent with {{aggregate()}}; however, users will generally not want to use {{aggregateCursor()}} with pipelines ending in {{$out}}, since the command will not execute until the cursor is rewound and the result set will be empty anyway.

summary: aggregate() with var args does not restore read pref after forcing primary for $out
Force primary read pref over primaryPreferred if pipeline ends with $out
description:
The intention of forcing a primary read preference for pipelines ending in {{$out}} was the help users avoid a command error if a secondary were to receive the command. We also emit a warning when doing so in order to notify them that their code _could_ fail.

With primaryPreferred, it's still possible for the command to be sent to a secondary. Therefore, we should also force a primary read pref over primaryPreferred to be consistent.

summary: Clear tag sets when forcing primary read pref for aggregate
description:
When the aggregate command helpers detect {{$out}} in the pipeline and force a primary read pref, they only set the type. Any existing read preference tags are left in place, which causes {{mongo_filter_candidates_by_tagset()}} to disregard the primary if it does not match the tag sets.

Overriding the read pref for the collection ({{aggregate()}} or command cursor ({{aggregateCursor()}}) should never leave it in an invalid state. If we set a primary read pref, the tags should be cleared (even temporarily).

summary: explicitly null-terminate buffer written by gethostname()
description: Issue reported directly with a github PR: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/814

summary: Implement get/setReadPreference() for MongoCommandCursor
description: Additionally, the methods should be added to MongoCursorInterface.
